title: Hyperscalers rethink campus power strategy as AI clusters drive denser builds
summary: Operators are moving from generic growth plans to site-specific power and substation strategies as AI density raises both time-to-power and capex sensitivity.
insight: Expert lens: The winners here will be teams that lock utility access, transformer slots, and cooling design before they finalize GPU procurement. In AI campuses, power lead time is now a market-share variable.

title: Liquid cooling shifts from pilot projects to standard rack design in new AI halls
summary: Facilities teams are standardizing CDU, rear-door, and liquid loop decisions earlier in the design cycle instead of treating them as late-stage retrofits.
insight: Expert lens: Cooling is no longer a mechanical afterthought. It directly affects rack density, fit-out sequencing, and how quickly operators can monetize contracted GPU demand.

title: Neocloud providers are bundling inference, networking, and bare metal into tighter enterprise offers
summary: The next wave of AI infrastructure competition is shifting toward packaging quality: predictable throughput, deployment speed, and managed network architecture.
insight: Expert lens: Raw GPU access is commoditizing. Margin will move to orchestration, fabric design, and the ability to turn contracted capacity into stable enterprise service levels.
